description: smoke test for xgboost using all_numeric
smoke test for xgboost adding low-cardinality categoricals via XGBoost native cat support
smoke test for xgboost adding commune_codes + cadastral_sections as native cats
deepen iter1: log1p target on geo_signal recipe
deepen iter2: derived ratios on geo_signal recipe
deepen iter3: presence flags on geo+ratios
deepen iter4: log1p of skewed numerics on geo+ratios
deepen iter5: transaction date ordinal
deepen iter6: room count + area aggregates
deepen iter7: avg area per apt/house
deepen iter8: fold-safe commune target encoding (smooth=20)
deepen iter9: drop sparse per-room/per-area columns
deepen iter10: re-add property_type as third native cat
tune iter1: lr=0.025, n_est=1000
tune iter2: max_depth=5
tune iter3: max_depth=6
tune iter4: min_child_weight=3
tune iter5: min_child_weight=10
tune iter6: subsample=0.7
tune iter7: subsample=0.9
tune iter8: colsample_bytree=0.7
tune iter9: reg_lambda=2.0
tune iter10: reg_lambda=4.0
ablate c1: drop max_depth=6 (revert to 4)
ablate c2: drop subsample=0.9 (revert to 0.8)
ablate c3: drop colsample=0.7 (revert to 0.8)
ablate c4: drop reg_lambda=2.0 (revert to 1.0)
confirm seed=2026: promoted recipe (max_depth=6 only)

Stage 1 smoke iter1: RandomForestRegressor(n_est=200,max_depth=18,mls=2,max_features=sqrt) on all_numeric lane (excl parcel_ids/transferred_parcel_ids). Fair anchor vs xgboost smoke iter1 (53600.06) and HGB iter1 (70431.74).
Smoke iter2: all_numeric + OHE(property_type, transaction_type)
Smoke iter3: all_numeric + 5 structural ratios (drops OHE cats from iter2)
Deepen iter1: add 5 structural ratios on top of cats baseline
Deepen iter2: add commune_first target encoding (smoothed=20, fold-safe)
Deepen iter3: add cadastral_first target encoding (smooth=20)
Deepen iter4: add transaction_date as date_ordinal (days since 2014-01-01)
Deepen iter5: REVERT date_ordinal, add commune frequency encoding (count of train records per commune)
Deepen iter6: add property_type target encoding (smooth=20) alongside OHE
Deepen iter7: log1p target -- HGB iter21 saw lift, xgboost saw none; RF with squared_error criterion may benefit
Deepen iter8: add transaction_type target encoding (smooth=20) alongside OHE
Deepen iter9: add 5 presence flags (has_apt, has_house, has_land, has_commercial, has_built)
Deepen iter10: drop OHE cats; rely solely on target_enc + presence_flags for cat signal (simplification test)
Tune iter1: criterion=absolute_error -- direct loss-metric alignment for sklearn RF. Note ~10x slower split computation expected.
Tune iter2: n_estimators=500 (was 200) -- more trees for better averaging. Will be slower.
Tune iter3: REVERT n_est to 200, set max_depth=None (unlimited)
Tune iter4: min_samples_leaf=1 (was 2)
Tune iter5: REVERT mls=2; max_features=0.5 (was sqrt)
Tune iter6: max_features=0.7
Tune iter7: REVERT mf=0.5; bootstrap=False (pasting)
Tune iter8: max_features=0.4 (was 0.5)
Tune iter9: target_enc smooth=10 (was 20)
Tune iter10: target_enc smooth=5 (was 10)
Ablate c1: revert criterion to squared_error
Ablate c2: revert max_depth to 18 (NEW family best -- max_depth=None was harmful)
Ablate c3: revert max_features to sqrt
Ablate c4: revert bootstrap=True
Confirm seed=2026 of promoted simplified recipe (W minus max_depth=None)
Stage 1 smoke iter1: 4 cols with strongest solo signal (single-col CV MAE improvement >3500 vs median baseline 92674)
Stage 1 smoke iter2: 4 cols with mid-tier solo signal (single-col CV MAE improvement 0-1400 vs median baseline 92674). Disjoint from tier1.
Stage 1 smoke iter3: long-tail of weak/negative solo cols; tests compositional signal. Excludes constants and transaction_date.
Deepen iter1: + commune_first (first token of commune_codes) as native xgboost categorical
Deepen iter2: + cadastral_first (first token) as native cat. Carry: commune_first.
Deepen iter3: + transaction_type as native cat. Carry: commune_first, cadastral_first.
Deepen iter4: + land_area, num_lots, num_premises. Carry: tier1 + commune_first + cadastral_first + transaction_type.
Deepen iter5: + entity counts (num_houses, num_apartments, num_commercial).
Deepen iter6: + room layout cluster as one knob (all area_house_*, num_house_*, area_apt_*, num_apt_*, plus apartment_area, num_house_5plus_rooms).
Deepen iter7: + date_ordinal.
Deepen iter8: log1p target wrap; predict in log space, inverse with expm1.
Deepen iter9: + built_per_premise, land_per_lot, commercial_share, apt_share, houses_per_premise.
Deepen iter10: + commune_first_target_enc (smoothed mean, smooth=20). Encoder fit on train fold; applied to val. commune_first kept as native cat too.
Stage 3 tune iter1: max_depth=8 (was 6); deeper trees on top of W (deepen iter9).
Stage 3 tune iter2: max_depth=4 (was 6); shallower.
Stage 3 tune iter3: lr=0.03 + n_est=1000 (capacity_pair: paired tweak; effective compute similar).
Stage 3 tune iter4: min_child_weight=2 (was 5); less leaf regularization.
Stage 3 tune iter5: min_child_weight=10.
Stage 3 tune iter6: subsample=0.6.
Stage 3 tune iter7: colsample_bytree=0.6.
Stage 3 tune iter8: colsample_bytree=1.0; test other direction.
Stage 3 tune iter9: reg_lambda=5.0; more L2 reg.
Stage 3 tune iter10: reg_alpha=1.0; add L1 reg.
Ablate a1: LOO remove commune_first from cats; test if it is load-bearing.
Ablate a2: LOO remove cadastral_first.
Ablate a3: LOO drop the 3 raw count/area cols added in deepen iter4.
Ablate a4: LOO disable log1p target wrap.
Ablate a5: LOO drop date_ordinal.
Ablate a6: combined removal of log1p target wrap AND date_ordinal feature; verify simplified W''.
Stage 4 confirm seed=2026 of W' (tune_iter7 colsample=0.6 + all kept knobs).

Smoke iter1: XGBoost on ONLY the Ridge baseline's 3 features (built_area, num_lots, num_commercial). Family premise -- the hypothesis IS the feature set, so a single smoke lane is correct here; multi-lane smoke would defeat the purpose of testing this exact constraint. Compare CV MAE vs xgboost_v2 promoted (49247) and Ridge baseline.
Deepen iter1: wrap target with log1p (expm1 inverse). xgboost_v2 found this tie-positive (-322); test if it ports under feature-starved 3-feature regime.

Deepen iter2: monotone_constraints={built_area:+1, num_lots:+1, num_commercial:+1}. Domain knowledge: more area/lots/commercial units => higher value. Should reduce overfitting noise when the model is feature-starved.
Deepen iter3: clip y_train at 99.5th percentile during fit (cap extreme outliers); MAE eval still uses raw y. Tail outliers may be hurting MAE in feature-starved 3-feature regime.
Deepen iter4 (capacity-knob): max_depth=4 (vs 6). With 3 features, depth-6 trees may overfit interactions. Less capacity = stronger generalization in feature-starved regime.
Deepen iter5: lr=0.03 + n_est=1000 (capacity-pair, slower learning, more trees). Helps converge to a finer optimum on a feature-starved 3-feature problem.
Deepen iter6: colsample_bytree=1.0 (vs 0.8). With only 3 features, 0.8 samples ~2 cols/tree = throws away info every tree.
Deepen iter7: subsample=1.0 (vs 0.8). Drop row-sampling randomness; with feature-starved 3 cols the variance from sampling may not pay for itself.
Deepen iter8: max_depth=3 (vs 4). Push Occam direction further -- with 3 features depth 3 may regularize even better.
Deepen iter9: max_depth=2 (vs 3). Test Occam ceiling -- tiny trees, max info per split with 3 features.
Deepen iter10: lr=0.1 (vs 0.05). With shallow depth=2 trees, faster learning may find a better optimum within 500 iters.
Ablate a1: revert max_depth to default 6 (keep colsample=1.0). Test whether max_depth=2 was load-bearing vs W'''.
Ablate a2: revert colsample_bytree to default 0.8 (keep max_depth=2). Test whether colsample=1.0 was load-bearing vs W'''.
Stage 4 confirm seed=2026 of W''' (deepen iter9 winner: max_depth=2 + colsample=1.0). Verify seed agreement before promotion.
Smoke Lane A: v2 base + dept_code (native cat) + region_code (native cat). Tests whether geographic hierarchy above commune adds signal not captured by commune_first cat alone.
Smoke Lane B: v2 base + year (native cat) + month (native cat) + future_sale (numeric). Tests temporal granularity beyond date_ordinal.

Smoke Lane C: v2 base + fold-safe target encoding on commune_first (smooth=30). TE computed on train fold only, applied to validation. Adds smoothed mean(log1p(value)) signal beyond native cat encoding.
Deepen iter1: add num_communes, num_sections, num_parcels (geographic granularity counts). Single idea: properties spanning multiple geographic units may have different value distributions.
Deepen iter2: add fold-safe dept-level TE (smooth=20). 96 dept levels = much more reliable than 36k commune levels; commune TE failed but dept TE may aggregate signal at the right granularity.
Deepen iter3: add built_per_land = built_area/land_area (terrain density). Trees struggle with explicit ratios; this captures urban-vs-rural density even when both inputs are present.
Deepen iter4: add total_rooms = sum of all num_*_rooms columns. Different size proxy than area features.
Deepen iter5: add fold-safe built_rel_type = built_area / mean(built_area|property_type) computed from train. Measures property size relative to type norm.
Deepen iter6: add fold-safe built_rel_commune = built_area / mean(built_area|commune_first). Measures size relative to local market norm.
Deepen iter7: add fold-safe built_rel_dept = built_area / mean(built_area|dept_code). 96 dept levels means more reliable group means than commune-level.
Deepen iter8: add fold-safe land_rel_type = land_area / mean(land_area|property_type). Analogous to iter5 winner but for land area.
Deepen iter9: add fold-safe TE on property_type (smooth=20). Low cardinality (~10 types) gives stable means; trees can use the continuous TE complementarily to the cat.
Deepen iter10: drop dept_code and region_code native cats. Test whether they're load-bearing in W''.
Deepen iter11: add fold-safe house_area_rel_type = house_area / mean(house_area|property_type). Replicate iter5 winner pattern for second-most-important area feature.
Deepen iter12: predict by averaging 3 XGBoost models with seeds 42, 100, 200. Variance reduction often gives consistent small lift orthogonal to feature engineering.
Deepen iter13: max_depth=8 (vs 6). v4 has more features than v2; deeper trees may catch new interactions.
Deepen iter14: predict by averaging log-target and raw-target XGBoost predictions (50/50). Different target scales = different residual structures, often additive lift.
Deepen iter15: add type_mean_built = mean(built_area | property_type) per row, fold-safe. Direct group-stat (not ratio) telling model the typical built_area for the type.
Deepen iter16 (tune): lr=0.03 + n_est=1000. Slower learning, more iterations may extract more signal from current feature set.
Deepen iter17: train with internal 90/10 train/eval split + early_stopping_rounds=30 + n_est=2000. Picks optimal stopping point per fold.
Deepen iter18: 5-model heterogeneous ensemble averaging (vary max_depth, lr, subsample). Greater diversity than seed averaging.
Deepen iter19: stack Ridge regression on XGB residuals. Captures linear patterns the boosted trees miss.
Deepen iter20: drop cadastral_first cat. Cad sections nest within communes; may be redundant with commune_first cat.
Deepen iter21: 2D TE on (property_type, dept_code) with smoothing=30. Captures type-specific regional pricing differences.
Deepen iter22: train global + 2 per-type sub-models (UNE MAISON, UN APPARTEMENT). Predict global. For MAISON/APPARTEMENT rows, blend 0.5 global + 0.5 sub-model. Type-specific specialization.
Deepen iter23: extend per-type sub-models to top 5 types: UNE MAISON, UN APPARTEMENT, TERRAIN DE TYPE TERRE ET PRE, TERRAIN DE TYPE TAB, ACTIVITE. Same 0.5 blend.
Deepen iter24: blend ratio 0.7 sub / 0.3 global (vs 0.5/0.5). Sub-models may have stronger type signal than global.
Deepen iter25: blend ratio 0.3 sub / 0.7 global. Less sub-model influence; global may be more reliable for type-specific patterns it already learned.
Deepen iter26: sub-models trained with max_depth=4 (vs global's 6). Smaller per-type datasets benefit from more regularization.
Deepen iter27: per-type sub-models trained on RESIDUALS (target - global_pred) instead of raw target. Final pred = global + sub_residual.
Deepen iter28: per-type sub-models train on log-residuals (log_y - log_pred_global). Combine target skewness handling with stacking.
Deepen iter29: sub-models with n_est=300 (vs global's 500). Smaller per-type datasets need less capacity.
Deepen iter30: gamma=1.0 (vs default 0). Min split gain regularization. Untested in v2 search.
Deepen iter31: drop num_/area_*_rooms (18 sparse cols). May reduce noise.
Deepen iter32: replace global with average of 5 XGB models on bootstrap samples (with replacement). Per-type sub-models stay same. Bootstrap diversity > seed diversity.
Deepen iter33: n_estimators=1000 (vs 500), keep lr=0.05. More trees may extract more signal.
Deepen iter34: n_estimators=1500 (vs 1000). More trees, see if lift continues.
Deepen iter35: n_estimators=2000 (vs 1500). Continue exploring capacity ceiling.
Deepen iter36: n_estimators=3000. Push trees further if lift continues.
Deepen iter37: n_estimators=4000. Test if returns continue diminishing.
Deepen iter38: n_estimators=5000. Push capacity ceiling further.
Deepen iter39: lr=0.03 + n_est=4000. Slower learning may extract more signal at fixed tree budget.
Deepen iter40: max_depth=8 with n_est=4000. Test depth at higher tree count.
Ablate a1: drop per-type sub-models (no SUB_TYPES). Test if the per-type architecture is load-bearing in W'''''.
Stage 4 confirm seed=2026 of W''''' (iter37 winner: per-type sub-models + n_est=4000 + all kept knobs). Validate seed agreement before promotion.

Smoke Lane A: v4 W''''' base + presence flags (binary is_present indicators) for top-importance numeric fields (built_area, house_area, land_area, apartment_area, num_premises, num_houses, num_apartments). Tests whether missingness PATTERN carries signal beyond xgboost's native missing handling.

Smoke Lane B: v4 W''''' base + bounded-derived id token counts: num_parcel_ids = count(comma tokens in parcel_ids) and num_transferred_parcel_ids. Captures transaction scale/complexity orthogonal to area features. No raw IDs used.

Smoke Lane C: v4 W''''' base + commune_first_freq (fold-safe count of commune_first level in train fold). Distinct from commune-level TE (which failed Lane C in v4 due to noisy 36k means) — frequency signal measures market liquidity, not mean price. Trees can use it to weight reliability of cat-derived signals.

Deepen iter4: add fold-safe cadastral_first TE smooth=20 to v4 anchor. Section level (846 levels) is the untested middle ground between commune (36k means failed, v4 Lane C) and dept (96 means tied, v4 iter2). Best chance for TE to clear noise at this cardinality.

Deepen iter5: 3-seed averaging on v4 anchor. Replace single-seed predict with mean of seeds {42, 100, 200} for both global and per-type sub-models. Variance reduction. Distinct from v4 iter12 (3-seed at n_est=500); v5 anchor n_est=4000 has cv_mae_std ~5244 (vs v4 iter11 std ~4922), so seed effect may now exceed noise band.

Deepen iter6: replace per-type sub-models with per-region sub-models (top 5 regions by sample count). Tests whether geographic specialization captures more signal than property-type specialization. Same cost as anchor (still 6 model fits per fold).

Deepen iter7: per-type sub-models trained with max_depth=8 (vs global's max_depth=6). Sub-models see smaller per-type slices; v4 iter26 tested smaller sub-model depth=4 and tied. Untested direction is BIGGER sub-model capacity to capture richer type-specific interactions.

Deepen iter8: blend v4 anchor predictions 50/50 with HistGradientBoostingRegressor(absolute_error). Tests cross-family model diversity -- HGB and XGB have different histogramming and split criteria. v4 iter18 heterogeneous-XGB ensemble failed at n_est=500; this is cross-family blend at v4 capacity.

Deepen iter9: extend per-type sub-models from top 5 to top 10 types. v4 iter23 extended from 2 to 5 (-57 lift); the next-tier types may have enough samples to add similar marginal lift even though smaller.

Deepen iter10: ON TOP OF iter9 (top-10 sub-models, KEPT), bump n_estimators 4000->5000 in BOTH global and sub-models. v4 iter38 tested n_est=5000 alone (-66 lift, within noise); compounding with iter9's -130 may clear noise band cumulatively.

Deepen iter11: ON TOP OF iter10 (top10 sub-models + n_est=5000 KEPT), test mechanically-paired knob lr=0.04 + n_est=6250 (lr*n_est=250 same step budget as iter10's 0.05*5000=250). Slower learning at fixed step budget often extracts more signal.

Deepen iter12: ON TOP OF iter10 (top10 + n_est=5000 KEPT), change BLEND ratio 0.3 -> 0.4 for sub-model contribution. v4 ablate showed sub-models contribute marginally (+285 when dropped); raising blend weight tests whether more sub-model influence helps now that there are 10 sub-models (vs v4's top 5). Single knob.

Deepen iter13: ON TOP OF iter10 (top10 + n_est=5000), test subsample 0.8 -> 0.7 (single knob). Untested in xgboost_v4 chain. Lower subsample = more variance per tree = potentially better generalization in saturated capacity regime.
Stage 4 confirm seed=2026 of iter10 (top10 sub-models + n_est=5000 on v4 anchor). Two-seed agreement check before any global-champion claim.

Smoke Lane A: v5 iter10 anchor + bounded numeric components parsed from first parcel_id (prefix code and parcel number). Train-only inspection found structured cadastral IDs; tests whether within-section parcel positioning carries signal beyond commune_first/cadastral_first cats without using raw high-cardinality IDs.

v2 Smoke lane A: v1 promoted recipe + fold-safe built_rel_type = built_area / mean(built_area | property_type). Anchor = v1 promoted recipe (50663.64 seed=42). xgboost_v4 saw -245 from this exact unit.

v2 Smoke lane B: v1 promoted recipe + parcel_id_count and transferred_parcel_id_count (comma-split lengths). Anchor v1 (50663.64). Hypothesis: ID tokens encode multi-parcel structure; bounded counts dodge the high-cardinality leakage warning in feature_search_space.md.

v2 Smoke lane C: v1 promoted recipe + 10 room density ratios (area_*/num_* per apt/house room bucket). Anchor v1 (50663.64). Hypothesis: per-room area is a per-dwelling size signal that aggregate built_area misses; protected against zero-num denominators.
v2 Deepen iter1: lane A best (built_rel_type) + built_per_land = built_area/land_area cumulative. xgboost_v4 saw -116 from this exact unit.
v2 Deepen iter2: lane A (built_rel_type) + room_area_density_ratios cumulative. Lane C alone showed -88 vs anchor; cumulative may push -180 if the two signals are non-redundant.

v2 Deepen iter3: ExtraTreesRegressor swap on lane A (built_rel_type). Same hyperparams (criterion=absolute_error, n_est=200, depth=18, mls=2, mf=0.4, bootstrap=False). Random splits trade some bias for less-correlated trees + variance reduction; might reach a noise-aware lift where features alone plateaued.
v2 Deepen iter4: capacity bump n_est 200 -> 400 on lane A. v1 tune iter2 (n_est=500) was within noise but with depth=None; depth=18 + bagging=False trees are smaller and benefit from more averaging.

v2 Deepen iter5: max_depth 18 -> 22 on lane A. v1 ablate found depth=None harmful and depth=18 the sweet spot, but local search beyond 18 wasn't done. With built_rel_type now in the feature mix, a slightly deeper tree may capture missed type-x-size interactions.

v2 Deepen iter6: min_samples_leaf 2 -> 1 with depth=18 fixed. v1 tested mls=1 with depth=None and saw regression -- but with depth=18 cap, mls=1 may give finer terminal granularity without overfitting.

v2 Deepen iter7: per-property-type sub-model architecture on lane A. Train top-3 type sub-RFs (n_est=100, same recipe) on type subsets of train; blend 0.3*sub + 0.7*global for predictions of that type. xgboost_v4 saw -215 cumulative from this exact architecture. Top types: UNE MAISON (10085), UN APPARTEMENT (6443), TERRAIN DE TYPE TERRE ET PRE (2020).

v2 Deepen iter8: add log1p of 9 heavily-skewed numeric columns (areas + count-of-X). Hypothesis: depth=18 trees may struggle to find split points in heavy-tailed distributions; explicit log transforms give RF easier-to-split linear-scaled features. Untried lift in this family.

Smoke Lane B: v4 W''''' anchor + bounded numeric components parsed from first parcel_id (prefix code and parcel number). Tests whether within-section parcel positioning adds signal to the promoted v4 recipe without using raw high-cardinality IDs.

v2 Deepen iter9: target_enc smooth 10 -> 15 on lane A. Mid-point between v1's tested 10 (winner) and 20 (baseline). With built_rel_type added in lane A, the optimal smoothing may have shifted slightly higher.

v2 Deepen iter10: max_features 0.4 -> 0.3 on lane A. v1 tested 0.5/0.7/sqrt + 0.4 won. Below 0.4 untested; more aggressive feature decorrelation between trees may help with the built_rel_type addition.

v2 Deepen iter11: lane A + built_rel_commune = built_area / mean(built_area | commune_first), fold-safe. Different group than built_rel_type (commune ~196 vs property_type ~25); injects geographic-relative size signal.

Smoke Lane C: v4 W''''' anchor + combined commune_section categorical (commune_first + cadastral_first). Tests geo_granularity: section codes repeat across communes, so a nested combined category may preserve cadastral geography that separate native cats only approximate.

Smoke Lane C: v4 W''''' anchor + combined commune_section categorical (commune_first + cadastral_first). Tests geo_granularity: section codes repeat across communes, so a nested combined category may preserve cadastral geography that separate native cats only approximate.

Deepen iter1: extend per-type sub-models from promoted v4 top 5 types to top 15 types, keeping n_estimators=4000 and blend=0.3. v5 top10 gave a small lift; this tests whether additional type coverage continues that direction as one architecture knob.

Deepen iter2: on top of iter1 (top15 per-type sub-models kept), increase n_estimators from 4000 to 5000 in both global and sub-models. v5's top10+n_est5000 step improved by ~69 MAE; repeating it on top15 may beat the raw global best.
Stage 4 confirm seed=2026 of iter2 (top15 per-type sub-models + n_est=5000). Verify the new raw-best recipe against the prior v4/v5 confirmation baselines before promotion.

Deepen iter3: on top of iter2 (top15+n_est5000), increase n_estimators from 5000 to 6000 in both global and sub-models. Tests whether the capacity ramp still has headroom after the new raw-best iter2.
Stage 4 confirm seed=2026 of iter3 (top15 per-type sub-models + n_est=6000). Verify the new raw global best before promotion.

Smoke Lane A: v6 promoted anchor + extend per-type sub-models from top15/min200 to top20/min100. Wide architecture coverage test: determine whether remaining mid-frequency property types add specialized signal before local tuning.

Smoke Lane B: v6 promoted anchor with submodel BLEND lowered from 0.3 to 0.2. Wide architecture-weight test: v6 gained from more type coverage, but less submodel weight may reduce variance while keeping type-specific signal.
Smoke Lane C: v6 promoted anchor with n_estimators increased from 6000 to 7000 in both global and sub-models. Wide capacity-axis test before any local deepen/tune loop.

Deepen iter1: after wide smoke selected the capacity lane, increase n_estimators from 7000 to 8000 in both global and top15 sub-models. Tests whether the v6/v7 capacity curve still has headroom before confirmation.

Smoke lane A retry: sklearn GradientBoostingRegressor(loss=absolute_error, n_est=100, lr=0.1, max_depth=3, subsample=0.8) on all_numeric (37 cols after dropping region_code, parcel_ids, transferred_parcel_ids, commune_codes, cadastral_sections). Drops applied to fix prior float32 overflow precondition crash.

Smoke lane B: lane-A anchor plus one-hot encoded property_type (25 cats) and transaction_type (6 cats). Tests whether low-cardinality property/transaction class signal lifts GBR over the numeric-only baseline. Single-knob: feature scope only; model hyperparameters unchanged.

Smoke lane C: lane-A anchor plus one fold-safe smoothed mean-target encoding of commune_codes (~196 unique). Encoding fit only on each fold's training split (k=20 smoothing toward global train mean, global fallback for unseen communes). Single-knob: feature scope only; model hyperparameters unchanged.
Stage 4 confirm seed=2026 of v7 iter1 (top15 per-type sub-models + n_est=8000). Verify the new raw global best before promotion.

Deepen iter3: max_depth 3 -> 4 on smoke geo_signal anchor. Tests interaction-depth bottleneck (vs iter2 capacity-count which tied). Single hyperparameter knob; n_estimators=100, lr=0.1, subsample=0.8 unchanged. If this also ties, capacity at this feature set is exhausted and iter4 should pivot to feature scope (cadastral TE or area_density_ratios).

Deepen iter4: max_depth 4 -> 5 chained on iter3 (new family best 54102.89). Single hyperparameter knob. Tests whether depth keeps paying after the clear iter3 win. If this ties, depth is saturated and iter5 pivots to feature scope.

Deepen iter5: anchor on iter3 (max_depth=4 family best). Add second fold-safe smoothed mean-target encoding column for cadastral_sections (846 unique, long-tail with 79% having <5 records). Single-knob: feature scope (+1 column). Same smoothing rule k=20. Hypothesis: section captures within-commune variation; smoothing handles the long tail.

Deepen iter6: anchor iter3 (max_depth=4 family best) + 3 size-normalized domain ratios as one hypothesis_unit -- built_area/num_premises, land_area/num_lots, num_commercial/num_premises (zero-safe via small-eps denominators). Single-knob (feature scope: 1 unit, 3 columns).
Deepen iter7: smoothing-k 20 -> 5 on commune target encoding. Single preprocessing knob (smoothing constant). Same encoding strategy, fold-safety, and feature shape; only the k value changes.

Deepen iter8: apply log1p to skewed area columns (land_area, built_area, house_area, apartment_area, all 10 area_apt_*_rooms / area_house_*_rooms). Single hypothesis_unit (log_skewed_numerics). Single preprocessing knob anchored on iter3. Hypothesis: tree splits on raw areas waste capacity on the long tail; log compression should make split thresholds carry more rows.
Deepen iter9: min_samples_leaf 1 -> 20 on iter3 anchor. Single hyperparameter knob. Hypothesis: enforces >=20 rows per leaf to reduce variance from small-leaf medians at max_depth=4.

Deepen iter10: subsample 0.8 -> 1.0 on iter3 anchor (deterministic gradient, no per-stage row sampling). Single hyperparameter knob. Hypothesis: stochastic gradient may add noise that doesn't help on this 27k-row dataset; full-row gradient may stabilize the boosting trajectory.

Deepen iter11: max_features None -> 'sqrt' on iter3 anchor. Single hyperparameter knob (column subsampling per split). Hypothesis: sqrt(38)~6 features per split decorrelates trees, may reduce variance even though sklearn GBR is gradient (not bagging) -- useful in a per-stage stochastic model with subsample=0.8.

Deepen iter12: drop the 20 sparse room-layout columns (10 num_apt/house_*_rooms + 10 area_apt/house_*_rooms) on iter3 anchor. Each column is mostly 0 because rooms are gated by property_type. Single-knob (feature scope: drop unit). Hypothesis: removing sparse-zero columns reduces noise without losing signal that house_area / apartment_area / built_area already encode at the aggregate level.

Deepen iter13: n_estimators 100 -> 200 alone on iter3 anchor (no lr change). Different from iter2's mechanically-paired bump. Single hyperparameter knob. Hypothesis: more boosting stages at the same step size may keep refining residuals; iter2's paired bump halved lr too aggressively.

Deepen iter14: n_estimators 200 -> 300 chained on iter13 (n_est=200 mean+std win). Single hyperparameter knob. Hypothesis: if 200 trees lifted both mean and std, 300 may keep paying before overfitting; observe std as the key diagnostic.

Deepen iter15: max_depth 4 -> 5 chained on iter13 (n_est=200 family best). Single hyperparameter knob. Hypothesis: iter4 (depth=5 on n_est=100) showed std rise; with n_est=200 the per-tree contribution is smaller (lr same, more stages) so depth=5 may now be better-behaved. Closes the user-requested upper-budget iter11-15.

Deepen iter2: on top of promoted v7 top15 plus n_est=8000, replace fixed 8000 trees with n_estimators=12000 and fold-internal early_stopping_rounds=200. The internal eval split is drawn only from each outer training split; the outer validation fold remains untouched.
Smoke laneA: extend numeric scope by adding 4 omitted cadastral counts (num_parcels, num_sections, num_communes, num_dependencies) on top of v7 promoted recipe. Single feature knob.
Smoke laneB: add 10 avg-area-per-room ratios (apt+house x 5 buckets) on top of v7 promoted recipe. Single feature knob.
Smoke laneC: drop 8 sparse 1-room/2-room layout columns from v7 promoted recipe. Single feature knob.
Deepen iter1: restore capacity (n_est 4000->8000, top_k 10->15) on lane B (room_area_density_ratios) winner. Establishes restored-capacity reference for v8 deepen anchor.

Deepen iter2: discard direction. DROP all 10 room_area_density_ratios from iter1's NUMERIC_COLUMNS. Single feature knob. Tests whether iter1's +1733 regression vs v7 promoted is attributable to those 10 columns. Also confirms v7-promoted reproducibility under v8 family pipeline.

Deepen iter3: anchored on iter2 (v7-equivalent). DROP 8 sparse 1-room/2-room raw cols (num_apt_1_room, area_apt_1_room, num_apt_2_rooms, area_apt_2_rooms, num_house_1_room, area_house_1_room, num_house_2_rooms, area_house_2_rooms). Single feature knob.
Deepen iter4: anchored on iter3 (drop sparse + no density). ADD num_parcels, num_sections, num_communes, num_dependencies. Single feature knob from iter3.

Deepen iter5: pivot back to family-best iter2 anchor. DROP dept_code and region_code from CATEGORICAL_COLUMNS (CLAUDE.md flags both as constant in training; they only add encoding overhead with no decision splits).

Deepen iter6: anchored on family-best iter2. Replace built_area, land_area, house_area, apartment_area with their log1p variants in NUMERIC_COLUMNS. Single preprocessing knob. Tests whether log spacing maps long-tailed area distributions onto more uniform hist-bin density.
Deepen iter7: anchored on iter6 (=iter2). ADD 5 binary presence flags (has_apartments, has_houses, has_commercial, has_dependencies, has_lots). Single feature knob.
Deepen iter8: anchored on iter7. ADD 4 cadastral density ratios (parcels_per_section, parcels_per_commune, sections_per_commune, lots_per_parcel). Single feature knob.
Deepen iter9: anchored on iter7 (family-best). ADD sin_month and cos_month cyclical encoding. Single feature knob.
Deepen iter10: anchored on iter7 (family-best). DROP 8 sparse 1-room/2-room raw cols. Single feature knob. Closes deepen at min=10.
Tune iter1: smoke-shrink anchor (iter7 recipe at n_est=4000, top_k=10). Single hyperparameter knob: learning_rate 0.05 -> 0.03.
Tune: tune_iter2_lr_007 on iter7 recipe at smoke-shrink (n_est=4000, top_k=10).
Tune: tune_iter3_max_depth_5 on iter7 recipe at smoke-shrink (n_est=4000, top_k=10).
Tune: tune_iter4_max_depth_7 on iter7 recipe at smoke-shrink (n_est=4000, top_k=10).
Tune: tune_iter5_mcw_3 on iter7 recipe at smoke-shrink (n_est=4000, top_k=10).
Tune: tune_iter6_mcw_10 on iter7 recipe at smoke-shrink (n_est=4000, top_k=10).
Tune: tune_iter7_subsample_07 on iter7 recipe at smoke-shrink (n_est=4000, top_k=10).
Tune: tune_iter8_colsample_05 on iter7 recipe at smoke-shrink (n_est=4000, top_k=10).
Tune: tune_iter9_reg_lambda_5 on iter7 recipe at smoke-shrink (n_est=4000, top_k=10).
Tune: tune_iter10_blend_02 on iter7 recipe at smoke-shrink (n_est=4000, top_k=10).
Final candidate seed=42: tune-best (lr=0.07) + iter7 features (presence_flags) at RESTORED capacity (n_est=8000, top_k=15).
Final candidate seed=2026: same recipe as final_seed42 (lr=0.07 + iter7 features + restored capacity), CV split seed=2026 for two-seed agreement.

Smoke iter1: XGBoost on top-3 target-correlated non-redundant features (built_area, num_lots, num_commercial) per mfm_cli analyze. First-principles 3-feature anchor analogous to baseline. Compare to xgboost_v3 baseline_three_only smoke (62674) and Ridge baseline.
Smoke iter2: XGBoost on pure-area triplet (built_area, land_area, house_area). Tests whether size dimensions alone carry signal vs Lane 1 (top-corr mixed counts+area).
Smoke iter3: XGBoost on pure-count triplet (num_lots, num_commercial, num_premises). Closes the first-principles smoke triad (mixed vs area-only vs count-only).

Deepen iter1: anchor for backward selection. 10-feature pool selected via mfm analyze (top non-redundant target correlations + redundancy-group reps). Subsequent iters drop one feature each (one-knob).
Deepen iter2: backward selection drop=built_area. Tests keystone hypothesis (smoke triad showed built_area dominant).
Deepen iter3: backward selection drop=land_area.
Deepen iter4: backward selection drop=house_area.
Deepen iter5: backward selection drop=apartment_area.
Deepen iter6: backward selection drop=num_lots.
Deepen iter7: backward selection drop=num_commercial.
Deepen iter8: backward selection drop=num_apt_2_rooms.
Deepen iter9: backward selection drop=num_house_2_rooms.
Deepen iter10: backward selection drop=year.
Deepen iter11: backward selection drop=month.
Deepen iter12: drop date_time unit (year+month together as one semantic group). Year and month single-drops were both within noise; testing them as a coherent unit.

Deepen iter13 (multi_change): keystone_pair_only = built_area + land_area. Tests whether the two backward-selection-essential features alone hold up vs the 10-feature anchor. Cumulative drop of all 8 tie-droppable features as a single recipe-level decision.

Deepen iter14 (multi_change): lean_7 = anchor minus the 3 strongest tie-droppers (month -26, num_apt_2_rooms -13, apartment_area +237). Tests cumulative drop of low-information features. After keystone_pair collapse (+4828) we know aggressive drops compound badly; this is the conservative middle ground.

Deepen iter15 (multi_change): lean_5 = essentials + top signal (built_area, land_area, house_area, num_lots, num_commercial). After lean_7 ties anchor, push to find floor by dropping num_house_2_rooms + year on top of lean_7's drops. If lean_5 ties, the 5-feature minimal is the recipe to confirm.

Smoke iter1 (signal isolation): XGBoost on 3 transfer-mode features (n_transferred, transfer_overlap_ratio, is_no_transfer) derived from parcel_ids vs transferred_parcel_ids set arithmetic. Tests whether the 32% no_transfer / 66% full_match / 1.5% partial split (median 80k vs 118k) predicts in isolation. Baseline-3-style.
Smoke iter2: re-establish v6 anchor at smoke speed (top5 sub-models, n_est=2000) without transfer features. Sets the baseline for measuring transfer-feature lift in Lane 3.

Smoke iter3: same shrunk v6 anchor as Lane 2 + 4 transfer-mode features (n_transferred, n_parcels_kept, transfer_overlap_ratio, is_no_transfer). Single-knob feature addition. Compare to Lane 2 (48441.69) for incremental lift.
Deepen iter1: re-anchor at n_est=1000 (vs Lane 2's 2000) so iter2 with transfer features fits the 10-min budget. Establishes the runtime-safe deepen baseline.

Deepen iter2 (single-knob feature addition): anchor n=1000 + 4 transfer-mode features (n_transferred, n_parcels_kept, transfer_overlap_ratio, is_no_transfer). Compare to iter1 (48772.08) for clean transfer-feature lift.

Smoke iter1 (signal isolation): XGBoost on 4 fold-safe commune-time velocity features (n_txns_prev_60d/180d, avg_built_area_prev_60d, days_since_last_txn). Tests whether commune-time density predicts in isolation. History built from training fold only; lookup strictly less-than row's own date.
Smoke iter2: re-establish v6 anchor at n_est=1000 (no velocity features) as v11 family baseline. Lane 3 will add velocity for lift measurement.

Smoke iter3: same anchor n=1000 + 4 fold-safe velocity features (n_txns_prev_60d/180d, avg_built_area_prev_60d, days_since_last_txn). Single-knob feature addition. Compare to Lane 2 (48772.08) for clean velocity lift.
New xgboost round: add transaction_type as a single native categorical feature on top of the promoted geo_signal recipe.
